story: The blazing round Sun hangs dead center of the window frame, one fierce dot pinned at its core, scorching the sill until noon — this is the brightest single DAY of summer.
reading_story: Nitch climbs onto the Sun's hot rim and roasts marshmallows there all DAY.

story: Across the empty desert stretches the Horizon Line — a single unbroken stroke where sand meets sky, and balanced on it is exactly ONE lonely tumbleweed.
reading_story: Itchy walks the whole Horizon Line and finds just ONE cactus to scratch his back on.

story: Inside a square Walled Box sits the King's Crown on a throne, and one glossy Ink Droplet falls beside it, stamping the royal seal that founds the whole COUNTRY.
reading_story: Coker patrols the Walled Box border, guarding the King's Crown so no rival seizes the COUNTRY.

story: A wooden Stepladder stands wide on its two splayed legs in the middle of the room — the only thing tall enough to count as a PERSON.
reading_story: Jin leans his guitar against the Stepladder, the one PERSON in the empty hall.

story: A Falling Katana slashes down through the flat Horizon Line above an Antenna Mast — the radio counts one more orbit around the sun, marking off a full YEAR.
reading_story: Nenn shimmies up the Antenna Mast each New YEAR to hang the countdown banner.

story: A bright orange Starfish sprawls across the rock, all five arms flung out as far as they reach, taking up the entire tide pool — impossibly BIG.
reading_story: Dyer measures the Starfish, gasps, and declares its span officially BIG.

story: Right where two straight roads cross at the Plus-Sign Crossroads, a hitchhiker counts the cars on each arm: exactly TEN have passed by sunset.
reading_story: Juno stands at the Plus-Sign Crossroads and tallies TEN buses before her lift arrives.

story: TWO steel Railway Tracks run side by side into the distance, perfectly parallel and evenly spaced — a pair of rails and nothing more.
reading_story: Knee trips on the Railway Tracks and scrapes both his knees — TWO of them.

story: A single Lightning Bolt cracks straight down and skewers an Open Mouth clean through its center — the bolt impales the very MIDDLE of the gaping square.
reading_story: Chewbacca catches the Lightning Bolt in his Open Mouth, right down the MIDDLE.

story: From the top of the banner a silk Trailing Tassel pours down the wall, its fine strands streaming endlessly toward the floor — astonishingly LONG.
reading_story: Cho braids the Trailing Tassel into her hair, and it grows ever so LONG.

story: A Falling Katana slashes down through a flat Horizon Line and buries its tip in a Mound of Soil, and from that wound a green shoot bursts up — raw life.
reading_story: The white-bearded Sage waters the sprouting shoot, coaxing fresh life (せい, Sage) from the soil.

story: The two heavy doors of a Castle Gate swing wide, and a single bright Sun hangs alone in the empty gap between them — the dead-center interval.
reading_story: Genghis Khan reins his horse in the gap, filling the interval (かん, Khan) of the gateway.

story: Inside a square Walled Box, the two stubby stone Wishing-Well Legs crouch, propping their bucket-frame up against all four high walls.
reading_story: Sheriff, the clipped lawman, counts off four outlaws boxed in the cell.

story: Now, a Pup-Tent Roof pitches its sharp ridge overhead while a single Horizon Line stretches flat beneath it, sheltering this exact moment.
reading_story: Sean Connery (Conn) checks his watch — the moment is now.

story: Gold: a single gleaming pyramid-topped Gold Ingot, two metal flecks glinting at its base, sitting heavy and bright on the table.
reading_story: King (Martin Luther) lifts the gleaming gold ingot overhead, きん, as the crowd roars.

story: Nine: a Curled Cat tucked into a tidy comma-shaped ball, tail flicking lazily — one creature reputed to carry nine lives.
reading_story: Governor Cuomo holds up nine fingers, きゅう, counting the curled cat's nine lives.

story: A canvas Tent Flap pinned open into an inverted V, daring you to duck low and enter underneath it.
reading_story: Paul Newman lifts the tent flap and steps inside, にゅう, ducking under the canvas.

story: A Pot Lid clamps the top, an Open Mouth gasps beneath it, and a Goalpost Frame caging a second Open Mouth holds the whole stack up — a tower stacked impossibly tall.
reading_story: Sebastian Coe sprints up the tower's endless stairs, panting at how TALL (こう, Coe) it climbs.

story: A Pot Lid rests on a Goalpost Frame; a flat Horizon Line floors it, a Lightning Bolt drops straight through the middle — a coin minted for one crisp yen.
reading_story: Enzo Ferrari flips a shiny one-yen coin, えん, and lets it spin on the table.

story: Child: a woven Swaddle Basket cradling a bundled baby, rocking gently in the nursery.
reading_story: Sheriff し leans over the swaddle basket and tips his hat to the sleeping child.

story: Three Bean Sprouts push up at the top, a Bedsheet Cover drapes the middle, and a Swaddle Basket sits below — a tiny scholar starting to study.
reading_story: Gackt the rock star cracks open a book to study, がく, sprouts and basket at his desk.

story: A forked Divining Twig held out under the thin orange Crescent Moon, the fortune-teller reading the night sky out beyond the walls, outside.
reading_story: Guy Fawkes slips outside under the crescent moon, がい, twig of fuse in hand.

story: A Speech Bubble on the left tilts toward a Lolling Tongue on the right, the dripping tongue flapping inside the bubble's words as the two never stop trying to talk.
reading_story: A clipped "Wah!" bursts from the tongue every time the bubble tries to talk (わ, Wah).

story: A Split Bed-Plank propped on the left and a Soup Ladle on the right stand stiffly back-to-back, both refusing to turn, pointing the lost campers due north.
reading_story: Hooker plants his rod on the icy shore, baiting his line while facing straight north into the wind.

story: A Falling Katana slashes down onto an Antenna Mast standing over a Plus-Sign Crossroads; the blade's shadow points dead straight with no lean, so it must be exactly noon.
reading_story: Goh blows a sharp whistle the instant the clock strikes noon.

story: A Writing Brush pressing down hard at the top drags fresh ink across a Sun glowing below it, every stroke the brush draws onto the disc forming a word — write.
reading_story: George Bernard Shaw dips the brush and writes (しょ, Shaw) glowing words across the sun.

story: Under a thin Crescent Moon, an Open Mouth below shouts up into the dark, calling out your name again and again until the moon finally answers back.
reading_story: May leans close and whispers your secret name so no one else hears it.

story: Three parallel channels of River Rapids rush straight downhill side by side, white water roaring between the steep banks, all one wild mountain river.
reading_story: Senna grips the wheel of a jet-boat, blasting full throttle down the foaming river.

story: A single Falling Katana slashes down over a Plus-Sign Crossroads, and where the blade strikes the road it shatters into a thousand gleaming shards scattering everywhere.
reading_story: Senna circles the track a thousand times, headlights streaking the night into one long blur.

story: A towering Tsunami Wave curls overhead, white spray flying off its crest as it hangs one terrible instant, then crashes down in a roaring wall of water.
reading_story: Swee takes a running leap off the dock and plunges headfirst into the cold water.

story: Twin Pillars lean apart at the top, Railway Tracks lie across them, and a Lightning Bolt cracks straight down to a Plus-Sign Crossroads below, splitting the whole tower in half.
reading_story: Hancock signs his huge flourish across only the top half of the page, leaving the rest blank.

story: Over a flooded Rice-Paddy Grid, a single Flexing Bicep strains beneath it, hauling the whole muddy field up by its ropes, the strongest working man in the village.
reading_story: Dan rolls up his sleeve and flexes, easily the strongest man at the worksite.

story: On the windowsill perches the Setting-Sun Cage, a round birdcage with the blood-red sun sinking behind its bars, its trapped bird watching the light vanish into the west.
reading_story: The white-bearded Sage turns to face west at dusk, bowing as the last light fades.

story: A Speech Bubble on the left, a Sheriff Star pinned above an Open Mouth on the right — the star-badged mouth recites, one bubble per phrase, a whole foreign language.
reading_story: Goh, badge on his chest, recites the star-mouth's foreign language (ご, Goh) word by word.

story: A single dark Mound of Soil sits heaped on the flat ground, a sprout-marker poked into its peak: soil.
reading_story: Homer 'Doh!' slaps his forehead as he face-plants straight into a fresh mound of soil.

story: The Sun blazes over a Mound of Soil on the left, while a Tape Measure on the right stretches across the dirt, ticking off inch after inch of time.
reading_story: The emcee Jee freestyles a tight countdown, calling out the exact time the beat drops.

story: A Falling Katana slashes down past a Mound of Soil, landing right between the Wishing-Well Legs that are already sprinting ahead of everyone.
reading_story: Ayrton Senna floors it to take the lead, always racing ahead of the pack.

story: One sturdy Tree stands alone, trunk straight, two branches reaching up and two roots spreading down: tree.
reading_story: Booker carves his initials deep into the bark of a towering tree.

story: A Tree grows up from one Horizon Line slicing across its lower trunk, and from that marked root sprouts a book.
reading_story: Honner cracks the spine of a fresh book and breathes in every printed page.

story: Behind a bare Tree the round Sun rises, a Lightning Bolt splitting the Horizon Line and lighting the Rice-Paddy Grid — dawn breaks in the east.
reading_story: Tone Loc raps to the rising sun, facing dead east at dawn.

story: Under a Pot Lid, Railway Tracks run down a Tree trunk struck by one Lightning Bolt, Scattered Rice Grains flung along the rails as the train comes.
reading_story: The Rye-field Catcher waves both arms, beckoning the whole crowd to come closer.

story: Both doors of a Castle Gate swing wide, and a single huge Listening Ear leans out through the gap, cupped forward, straining for the faintest sound to hear.
reading_story: Bunn presses his ear to the gate, straining to hear (ぶん, Bunn) the whisper inside.

story: A Heaped Rice Bowl steams under its just-lifted domed lid, rice mounded to a peak and begging you to eat.
reading_story: The Shocker wrestler tears into a giant turkey leg, daring you to eat with him.

story: A lone Cartwheel breaks off the wagon and rolls down the hill all by itself, bouncing over cobblestones, spokes whirring — somehow it has become an entire runaway car.
reading_story: Shaq folds his huge frame into a tiny car, knees at his ears, and revs it down the court — "sha!" — the car for the giant.

story: At the field's edge a stiff Scarecrow leans down over an Open Mouth shouting up at it, while a Meat Hook dangles between them snagging every reply. The whole scene asks: what?
reading_story: Cabbie Carr leans out his window — "ka?" — baffled, asking every passerby what street they actually want.

story: An Antenna Mast rises through a Plus-Sign Crossroads; below it Twin Pillars prop up a Goalpost Frame, all leaning toward the warm sun — the whole rig points dead south.
reading_story: The Nanny marches the kids due south for winter, clutching her "nan" umbrella against the warm sun ahead.

story: A Lightning Bolt stabs straight down, a Falling Katana slashes across it, and both shatter the flat Horizon Line into countless cracks numbering ten thousand.
reading_story: Author Thomas Mann scribbles "man!" beside a number with so many zeros it stretches to ten thousand.

story: One stark White Candle stands alone on the table, its tiny pale flame trembling, wax dripping down — everything it touches turns blindingly white.
reading_story: A Hacker in a white hoodie types "haku!" and floods every screen blinding white.

story: A flat Horizon Line lies across the desert, and on it a single White Candle stands burning — count its dripping wax beads and you reach a hundred.
reading_story: The Hyacker hijacks the abacus, sliding "hyaku!" beads across until they total a hundred.

story: Above the flat Horizon Line a giant orange Starfish floats, arms flung wide, sprawled across the empty blue — pinned high up there in the open sky.
reading_story: Detective Tennison tips his head back — "ten!" — and stares up into the wide open sky for clues.

story: A warm white Milk Jug brims on the counter, but an Iron Padlock is clamped over its handle, forbidding anyone to pour — only she may, your mother.
reading_story: Bo Derek cradles the warm milk jug like a baby, humming "bo," every inch the doting mother.

story: A Milk Jug pours fresh milk while an Iron Padlock snaps shut behind it, then springs open and snaps shut again — pour, lock, pour, lock, the same loop repeating morning after morning, every.
reading_story: Mai sets her alarm "mai!" and rises at dawn to pour the milk every single day.

story: A crackling Bonfire leaps up in tongues, sparks flying off both sides into the dark, branches popping — pure roaring fire.
reading_story: Cabbie Carr — "ka!" — slams his hood as the engine bursts into roaring fire.

story: A Falling Katana slashes down the top-left; its silver edge skids flat along the Horizon Line, then drops into a gaping Open Mouth that bites the blade off — pointing you to the right.
reading_story: Ooh, the crooner holds one long sung note as the katana points right.

story: A Speech Bubble on the left reads aloud to a War Helmet, draped in a Bedsheet Cover, propped on two Wishing-Well Legs — a swaddled ghost-knight made to read.
reading_story: Docker leans on the gate and reads (どく, Docker) the swaddled knight a bedtime tale.

story: A Falling Katana sweeps over a flat Horizon Line, and below-right a yellow Rubber Glove reaches up, palm open, to shake hands warmly with a friend.
reading_story: Yuna the figure skater glides over to greet a friend.

story: Reaching left, a Falling Katana sweeps down, scrapes along the flat Horizon Line, and clangs onto a red I-Beam Girder bolted in the lower-left corner.
reading_story: Sah, the clipped bare voice, barks the order to veer left.

story: Two woodsman's axes Crossed in a sharp X hang on the wall — the proud emblem your father mounted above the hearth.
reading_story: A Foo Fighter calls his father from backstage.

story: A Rain Cloud boxed at the top dumps its drops onto a Rice-Paddy Grid, and a barbed Fishhook dangling from the paddy's base sparks and crackles — pure electricity.
reading_story: Denny grips the sparking hook and a jolt of electricity (でん, Denny) throws him back.
